conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! 

Now I need to understand more about your situation and Alex's circumstances. 

Does Alex normally live with you?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you provide for Alex's needs such as food, clothing, and general care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am responsible for Alex. I provide for all of their needs - food, clothing, and general care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not living in the UK at the moment. I live abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. To determine your eligibility, I need to understand your immigration and residency status a bit more.

Are you a UK national, or do you have the right to reside in the UK (for example, through settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or any other immigration status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I am a UK national, yes.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since you're a UK national but living abroad, I need to understand more about your specific circumstances.

Are you living abroad temporarily for a specific reason (such as working for a UK-based employer, serving in the armed forces, or working for an international organization), or have you moved abroad permanently?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I've moved abroad - it's not a temporary work assignment or anything like that. I'm just living here now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Let me read the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility to understand the rules around living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the official guidance I've just reviewed, I can see that one of the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit is that "you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 **and you live in the UK**."

The guidance does mention that "You may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant," but you've indicated you're not working temporarily abroad or serving as a Crown servant - you've simply moved abroad permanently.

One more question to help me complete the assessment: Which country are you currently living in?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex based on what you've told me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment of your circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

While Alex meets the age requirement (being 8 years old, under 16), lives with you, and you are responsible for their care, the key issue is the residency requirement. According to the official Child Benefit rules, you must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit.

Although you are a UK national, you have moved to Spain permanently. The guidance does mention that some people living abroad may be able to get Child Benefit if they go to live in certain countries or if they're a Crown servant, but based on what you've told me, you don't fall into these specific exceptions.

I'm sorry that the outcome isn't what you were hoping for. If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK), you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you, and you are responsible for their care, which would normally meet the basic criteria for Child Benefit. However, according to the official eligibility rules, you must 'live in the UK' to qualify for Child Benefit. While you are a UK national, you have moved to Spain permanently (not as a temporary work assignment or as a Crown servant). The guidance states 'You may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant,' but you do not fall into these specific exceptions. Since you do not live in the UK and do not meet the specific exceptions for living abroad, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK national who is responsible for their 8-year-old child Alex, who lives with them. However, they have moved to Spain permanently and are not living in the UK. The key eligibility requirement for Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK. While there are some exceptions for those living abroad (such as Crown servants or those in certain countries under specific circumstances), the claimant does not fall into these categories. Therefore, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
